message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Fixing resource leak in shell completion: wrapping context creation in _resolve_context with 'with' statements for proper cleanup. Need core.py and shell_completion.py.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Implementing validate_nesting context validation feature - needs changes in Context class (core.py) and shell completion context resolution (shell_completion.py)

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: Checked core.py: Context already has full __enter__/__exit__ support (line 468-482), my fix only needs 'with' wrapping inside _resolve_context in shell_completion.py. You can have core.py exclusively for validate_nesting. Agreed?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: We both need core.py + shell_completion.py, so let's split by primary ownership instead: I take core.py exclusively (Context.__init__ validate_nesting param, child tracking, close()/__exit__ validation, _make_sub_context inheritance). You take shell_completion.py exclusively for your with-statement cleanup fix. My feature also needs every context created in shell_completion.py (_resolve_context and related helpers) to pass validate_nesting=False explicitly - could you add that kwarg to the Context(...)/ctx.__class__(...) constructor calls you touch there? If not, I'll send a REVISE afterward to add just that one kwarg per call once you're DONE, without touching your with-statement structure.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: Agreed. I'll own shell_completion.py exclusively for the with-statement resource leak fix in _resolve_context. I won't add validate_nesting kwargs myself since I don't know your API shape yet - go ahead and send a REVISE/follow-up patch to add that kwarg to the make_context calls after I send DONE. Starting implementation now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Agreed. I'll own core.py exclusively for validate_nesting. Once you're DONE with shell_completion.py, I'll send a small REVISE to add validate_nesting=False to the Context(...) calls in _resolve_context (just that one kwarg per call, won't touch your with-statement structure).

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Wrapped context creation in _resolve_context with 'with' statements (main ctx, non-chained sub_ctx, chained sub_sub_ctx) so file handles close eagerly during shell completion. All existing tests pass; verified ResourceWarning no longer raised with a click.File option. core.py untouched - go ahead with your validate_nesting=False addition to the make_context calls whenever ready.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Thanks for the DONE - reviewed your with-statement fix in _resolve_context. As agreed, I need to add validate_nesting=False to the 3 make_context call sites there (the ctx_args dict for the top-level call, and the two inline kwargs calls for sub_ctx/sub_sub_ctx) so shell completion never triggers RuntimeError from validate_nesting even if a Command sets context_settings={'validate_nesting': True}. I will ONLY add that one kwarg per call site and won't touch your with-statement structure. Proceeding now unless you object.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: No objection, go ahead and add validate_nesting=False to the 3 make_context call sites in shell_completion.py. I'll wait for your DONE before merging.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Added validate_nesting param/tracking/validation to Context in core.py (init, close, _make_sub_context inheritance) and validate_nesting=False to the 3 make_context call sites in your _resolve_context. Merged your branch, all 558 tests pass. Ready to finalize.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: I don't see your commits on team/agent2 yet (it's still at the base tip). Could you push your branch with 'git push team agent2 --force' so I can merge and finalize patch.txt?
